item.title: Passion Fruit 2-Pack — Panama Red & Bounty (Passiflora edulis) — Quick to Flower/Fruit! by PetioleAndBloomLLC
item.description:
24.99 USD

🍇 Passion Fruit 2-Pack — Panama Red & Bounty (Passiflora edulis)

🫶Our Panama Red vine FLOWERED in just 3 MONTHS and fruited right after! Follow our advice and yours can too! The flowers in the images are from our Panama Red plant this past summer.

Two beautiful passionfruit vines that work wonderfully together in your garden or on a trellis! This 2-pack pairs Panama Red and Bounty — two vigorous, edible passionfruit vines with showy flowers and delicious fruit that delight both growers and eaters alike. You’ll receive both plants with our starter fertilizer in the welcome kit to support growth through the first several months.

🎁 Welcome Kit Included!
We provide fertilizer you’ll need to support your vines through their first few months, plus care guidance and support — we’re here if you need help!

🌿 Passionfruit Pair Highlights

🟥 Panama Red Passion Fruit
A tropical, vigorous passionfruit vine known for its large, deep red to burgundy fruit with rich tropical flavor and dramatic ornamental flowers. Panama Red is self-fertile and flowers and fruits extremely well in warm, sunny conditions — growers often report blooms quickly after establishment, and with the right care you can expect fruit in its first season. Self-fertile passionfruit varieties like this often set fruit without a second pollen source.
Fruit color: Deep red/purple skin
Flavor: Sweet-tropical with citrusy/guava notes
Vigor: Fast growing and heavy cropping
Great for: Fresh eating, juicing, desserts
Best for: Full sun, strong trellis support
Instant gratification: With bright sun and good care, Panama Red can bloom and fruit in as few as a few months after planting — order now and follow our care advice for fruit this year!

🍇 Bounty Passion Fruit
A high-yield, purple passionfruit variety prized for its abundant fruit production and sweet-tangy flavor. It’s self-fertile and gets established quickly. While vigorous on its own, having Panama Red alongside can help pollinators stay active and improve overall flower activity in your garden.
Fruit color: Classic purple passionfruit
Flavor: Sweet, aromatic pulp perfect for fresh use
Yield: Very productive under the right conditions
Best for: Containers, patios, trellises

🍉 Do Panama Red and Bounty Cross-Pollinate?
Both Panama Red and Bounty are Passiflora edulis cultivars (purple/red forms of the species). Pollination in passionfruit is typically self-fertile, and both can set fruit on their own. However, planting two genetically distinct vines together can enhance pollinator activity and increase fruit set, especially if you encourage bees in your garden or hand-pollinate flowers.
Tip: For best fruit yields, ensure good bee visitation or gently hand-pollinate open flowers during peak bloom.

🍇 Quick Facts
Botanical Name: Passiflora edulis (both Panama Red & Bounty)
Growth: Fast-growing climbing vines
Light: Full sun for best flowering/fruiting
Support: Trellis, pergola, fence, arbor
Water: Even moisture with well-drained soil
Zones: Grows outdoors in warm climates; container culture further north

🌞 Growing Tips
Plant support first: Install a sturdy trellis before planting so vines can start climbing immediately.
Sun & soil: Passionfruit loves full sun and rich, well-draining soil.
Water consistently: Keep soil moist, not soggy.
Feed early: Use the starter fertilizer in the welcome kit to support growth through flowering and fruit set.
Encourage pollinators: Bees and insects help flowers set fruit; hand-pollination can make a big difference in some conditions.

🍴 Harvest & Uses
Both Panama Red and Bounty produce delicious tropical fruits that are perfect for:
Fresh eating
Juices and smoothies
Sauces, jams, desserts
Cocktails and flavor infusions
Their showy passion flowers also add beauty and attract pollinators long before fruit appears.

🌿 Why This 2-Pack Is Special
Two complementary passionfruit varieties with distinct flavor profiles
Great for beginners and experienced growers
Fast-flowering and fruiting potential when given sun and care
Excellent for both ornamental appeal and edible harvests

item.title: Vanilla Super Variegated Orchid | Actual Vanilla Bean Plant by PetioleAndBloomLLC
item.description:
19.99 USD

Vanilla planifolia ‘Super Variegated’ — 3″ Pot

🎁 Welcome Kit included to help you through the first several months

** Mix and Match, choose 2 or 3 and email with your preferred plants.

Why it’s awesome
🌿 Striking cream-and-green foliage—every plant is uniquely patterned
🫘 Yes, vanilla beans are possible with a mature plant, warm bright conditions, and hand pollination
🚿 Great bathroom plant—enjoys the extra humidity (window or small grow light = perfect)

What we do differently (so yours thrives)
✅ Most shops ship tiny plugs (which is totally fine)—we pot up to 3″ so you’re not nursing a baby plug. We'll send an established vine.
✅ We treat and refresh the media to encourage quick rooting and overall plant health
✅ Grown clean with good light and airflow for sturdy, ready-to-grow plants

What you’ll receive
One healthy Vanilla planifolia ‘Super Variegated’ in a 3″ pot (no stake yet)
Variegation varies—grower’s choice, same grade/quality as photos (natural variation is normal), reach out if you'd like to choose your own.

Easy care (TL;DR)
Light: Bright, indirect or lightly filtered; a touch brighter helps keep variegation (avoid harsh midday sun)
Water: Keep evenly moist—never soggy
Humidity: Loves a little extra + gentle airflow
Support: Add a moss pole/trellis as it grows so it can climb

Quick FAQ
Will it make beans? With time and the right setup—yes. Hand-pollinate blooms on a mature plant.
Will it stay variegated? Patterns naturally vary; give bright light and prune to favor the best-striped growth.

item.title: Lakatan “Golden” Banana Plant (Lacatan) | Premier Dessert Banana | Sweet, Creamy, Custardy | 3" pot | Well Established by PetioleAndBloomLLC
item.description:
18.99 USD

🍌✨ PREMIER DESSERT BANANA — LAKATAN (LACATAN)
✅ SWEET + AROMATIC • Creamy, dessert-like texture (“custardy” when perfectly ripe)
🪴 LIVE PLANT: Well-rooted starter established in a 3" pot
📦 ARRIVAL TIP: WATER ON ARRIVAL + GRADUAL SUN TRANSITION

Lakatan is a beloved dessert banana variety best known for its rich flavor and aroma. If you want something more interesting than a typical grocery-store banana, this is a standout choice for your patio, greenhouse, or tropical garden.

✅ YOU WILL RECEIVE:
• 1 LIVE Lakatan (Lacatan) banana plant
• Established in a 3" pot
• WELL-ROOTED in quality ammended soil and ready to grow

📸 PHOTOS:
Photos are of REAL plants from our collection (not stock images).
You will receive the same or similar plant with comparable health and vigor, feel free to reach out if you'd like to choose from available options.

⭐ WHY LAKATAN:
• Premium dessert-banana eating quality (sweet, aromatic, creamy)
• A favorite variety in the Philippines
• Great long-term container potential with warmth, light, and consistent feeding

🚚 ARRIVAL + FIRST WEEK (IMPORTANT):
• WATER IMMEDIATELY upon arrival.
• Keep out of harsh direct sun for ~7 days.
• Then transition gradually: bright light → morning sun → stronger sun over 1–2 weeks.

🌞 QUICK CARE:
LIGHT: Bright light indoors; gradual transition to stronger sun outdoors
WATER: Keep evenly moist (don’t let it fully dry out); avoid staying soggy
WARMTH: Loves heat + humidity; protect from cold and drafts
FEEDING: Bananas are heavy feeders — consistent nutrition = faster growth

🍌 FRUITING NOTE:
Fruiting depends on maturity, climate, container size, and long-term care. We can offer advice to ensure your plant thrives and to help you avoid many of the common mistakes!

📝 NAME NOTE (to prevent confusion):
“Lakatan” is also commonly spelled “Lacatan.” Some regions use “Lacatan” for other banana types, so we label ours as Lakatan (Lacatan).

item.title: Vanilla Orchid | Actual Vanilla Bean Plant by PetioleAndBloomLLC
item.description:
22.99 USD

Vanilla planifolia — Real Vanilla Orchid (3″ Pot)

🎁 Welcome Kit included to help you through the first several months!

*** Our longer vines have all sold out, we are working to grow more out, but it takes a few weeks. All of our plants are well rooted and healthy, and these vanilla plants do grow relatively quickly.

We are glad to substitute a super variegated orchid in a multipack if you like, just message right when you order as we sometimes pack an ship in very short order!

Why it’s awesome
🌿 Lush, tropical climber with glossy leaves
🫘 Yes, real vanilla beans are possible with a mature plant, warm bright conditions, and hand pollination
🚿 Great bathroom plant—thrives with extra humidity, especially near a window or under a small grow light

What we do differently (so yours thrives)
✅ Most shops ship a tiny plug still in acclimation—we pot up to 3" so you’re not nursing a baby plug and we ship longer vines than most! Well established!
✅ We treat with and refresh the media to encourage quick rooting and overall plant health
✅ Grown clean with good light and airflow for sturdy, ready-to-grow plants

What you’ll receive
One healthy Vanilla planifolia in a 3″ pot (no stake yet)
Grower’s choice—similar size/quality to photos (natural variation is normal), reach out if you'd like a choice!
Easy care (TL;DR)
Light: Bright, indirect or lightly filtered (windowed bathroom or grow light works great)
Water: Keep evenly moist—never soggy
Humidity: Loves a little extra + gentle airflow
Support: Add a moss pole/trellis as it grows so it can climb

⁉️Quick FAQ
Will it make beans? With time and the right setup—yes. Hand-pollinate blooms on a mature plant.
Good for homes? Absolutely—treat it like an orchid vine and give it something to climb.

item.title: Musa "Aurea" Variegated Lime Variegation, Collector Banana Plant, Edible Fruit Nono (Very Rare) by PetioleAndBloomLLC
item.description:
129.00 USD

VARIEGATED BANANA – MUSA AUREA 🌿
An EXTREMELY rare, eye-catching variegated banana with bold yellow, lime, and deep green striping. Each plant develops its own unique patterning, ranging from marbling and streaking to broader sectoral bands. New leaves may emerge more vivid and show stronger variegation as they mature.

Photos are of actual plants from our collection — not stock images.
You will receive the same or similar plant with comparable size, health, and variegation. Reach out if you'd like options to choose from.

Grown and prepared with collector standards in mind.
**Water immediately on arrival and adapt slowly to full sun if you're in a warmer climate that can support bananas outdoors.
WHAT’S INCLUDED 📦
Every plant ships with a care package designed to support it through its first several months, including:
• Detailed care instructions
• Banana-specific fertilizer
• Kelp to reduce stress in future transplanting
• Direct support--we LOVE banana plants and are glad to provide advice and support throughout the life of your plant. We are only a message away!

HOW OUR PLANTS ARE PREPARED
We take intentional steps to minimize stress and support long-term vigor before plants ever ship. Each plant is established in a well-draining, nutrient-rich soil blend rather than remaining in starter plugs.
Our growing mix includes organic and biological amendments such as kelp, marine-based nutrients, beneficial mycorrhizal fungi, and gentle, balanced fertilization. to support strong root development and reduced stress in shipping and future tranplanting.

ABOUT VARIEGATION
Variegated bananas are naturally dynamic plants.
• Variegation patterns may differ and can change over time
• Bright light helps maintain strong coloration
• Variegated plants may grow slightly slower than non-variegated bananas
Variegation is warranted for 30 days under proper care.

CARE OVERVIEW ☀️
• LIGHT: Bright light is essential. Indoors, place near a south-facing window and/or under strong full-spectrum grow lights. Outdoors, banana plants love full direct sun!
• WATER: Water deeply when the top inch of soil dries, allowing excess to drain
• TEMPERATURE: Prefers warm conditions; protect from cold
• ENVIRONMENT: Avoid sudden temperature changes and drafts
In cooler climates, this plant must be grown indoors.

ADDITIONAL NOTES
This banana type has been reported by growers to produce edible fruit under the right conditions, though fruiting is not guaranteed and depends on maturity, climate, and long-term care.
A great choice for banana collectors, variegated plant enthusiasts, and growers who enjoy watching plants develop over time.

item.title: Dwarf Green Banana Plant – Tropical Foliage, Edible Orange Fruit by PetioleAndBloomLLC
item.description:
24.99 USD

🍌 Dwarf Green Banana Plant (Musa ‘Dwarf Green’) — Sturdy Tropical Foliage & Edible
A lush, tropical dwarf banana with extremely thick deep green leaves and a compact, striking form that makes it a standout in any plant collection. Musa ‘Dwarf Green’ brings bold tropical energy to patios, sunny windows, greenhouses, and container gardens — and with the right care and bright conditions, it can produce creamy orange-pulp bananas that are both beautiful and delicious.

🎁 Welcome Kit with Every Plant!
We’re plant people, and we want your Dwarf Green to thrive! Every healthy plant ships with a care and transition packet, plus ongoing support — even long after your purchase.

💪 How Our Plants Are Different
Many sellers ship plants barely out of tissue culture in small, delicate plugs. We take a different ready-to-grow approach by shipping well-rooted plants in quality, naturally enriched soil that are primed to adapt and thrive.

We:
🌱 Dust the soil with DYNOMYCO at transplant to help establish strong roots.
💧 Follow with a gentle kelp + micronutrient treatment for balanced early growth.
🦀 Blend in natural slow-release nutrients to support soil biology and reduce transplant stress.
🌿 Condition each plant so it transitions smoothly into your home, patio, or greenhouse.
Result: vigorous roots, rich green foliage, and easier adaptation than typical freshly acclimated plugs.

🍌 Quick Facts
Botanical Name: Musa ‘Dwarf Green’
Mature Height: ~6 feet (compact for a banana plant)
Light: Bright light to full sun ideal
Water: Keep soil evenly moist, not soggy
Temperature: Loves warmth and humidity
Fruit: Can produce creamy orange-pulp bananas with proper heat and light •
Leaves: Thick, durable, excellent for wrapping, grilling, or serving food

🌿 What Makes It Special
Eye-Catching Tropical Foliage:
The broad, dramatic leaves create an instant tropical focal point — perfect for patios, greenhouses, and container gardens.
Exceptional Leaf Strength:
Dwarf Green is known for extremely thick, wind-tolerant leaves, making it tougher outside than many other banana plants.
Edible Fruit with Colorful Pulp:
This variety produces creamy orange-pulp bananas when given ample light, warmth, and nutrition — a delicious bonus that few dwarf bananas combine with such ornamental flair.
Culinary Leaf Uses:
The large, pliable leaves are ideal for cooking wraps, grilling, steaming, or serving food naturally — a feature that adds functional versatility to its beauty.

🌞 Growing Tips
Water immediately after arrival and keep soil evenly moist.
Acclimate to sunlight gradually, especially if moving from a lower-light environment to full sun.
Use a rich, well-draining mix and feed lightly during active growth.
Humidity helps — mist leaves occasionally if indoors.
Warm conditions and strong light encourage both lush foliage and fruiting.

📌 It will fruit in the right conditions
This plant is both ornamental and edible. With bright light, warmth, and good care, Dwarf Green can produce fruit that’s real, tasty, and cream-colored inside — a special experience for edible gardeners and tropical plant lovers alike.

item.title: Truly Tiny Banana Plant | Dwarf Musa Edible Fruit Producing (3" Pot) by PetioleAndBloomLLC
item.description:
21.99 USD

🍌 Truly Tiny Banana Plant (Musa ‘Truly Tiny’) — Compact Fruit Producing

A dwarf banana with lush, deep green leaves that stay darker than most varieties. Perfect for sunny windows, patios, or greenhouse collections, it reaches only 2–3 feet tall at maturity yet still produces small, edible bananas.

🎁 Welcome Kit with every plant! We want your plant to succeed!! We are plant people and we are always here if you need plant advice (even if you didn't get it from us!).

💪 How Our Plants Are Different
Many sellers ship plants barely out of tissue culture, still in their delicate acclimation plugs (which is totally fine).
We take a different approach by shipping well-rooted plants in quality, naturally enriched soil, ready to thrive.

We:
🌱 Dust the soil with DYNOMYCO at transplant to support healthy root growth .
💧 Follow with a light Spark + kelp treatment, jumpstarting growth and microbial balance for stronger roots and healthier leaves.
🦀 Blend in natural nutrients like crab & lobster meal — providing slow-release nutrition, supporting beneficial microbes, and helping reduce soil-borne pest naturally.
🌿 Condition each plant so it transitions easily to your home and next pot with minimal stress.
Result: stronger roots, deeper color, and faster adaptation than typical freshly acclimated plugs.

🍌 Quick Facts
Height: 2–3 ft (true dwarf)
Light: Bright indirect → full sun
Water: Keep soil evenly moist, not soggy
Temperature: 70–85°F preferred
Fruit: Small, edible bananas when mature
Growth: Compact tropical form ideal for pots or small greenhouses, perfect indoor plant banana (or outdoor spring/summer and indoor when temps drop).

🌞 Growing Tips:
Water immediately upon receipt!
Avoid direct sunlight for a full week then move into morning sun and progress to full sun over 1-2 weeks.
Repot when roots begin circling the pot (usually 2-4 weeks), don't change pots the first 1-2 weeks to avoid stressing the plant.
Use a well-draining mix and feed lightly every 2–4 weeks during active growth.
Never, ever, let the soil dry out... Bananas like moist soil and regular watering.
Loves humidity — mist occasionally or keep near other tropicals.
Avoid cold drafts; bananas slow dramatically below 65°F.

item.title: Banana Bunch | 4 Unique Musa Plants | Truly Tiny + Popo'ulu + Dwarf Green + Lakatan (Substitutions Permitted) by PetioleAndBloomLLC
item.description:
49.99 USD

A curated and established/well-rooted Banana Bunch featuring four uncommon Musa varieties (you won't find in the store) — perfect for collectors and tropical plant lovers 🌿🍌

What You’ll Receive:

• 4 established banana plants (one of each variety listed)
• Grown in 3-inch nursery pots
• Strong, healthy root systems
• Ready to uppot
• Complimentary Welcome Kit with each plant 🌱

Each plant is actively growing and well-rooted, prepared for its next stage in a larger container.

🍌 Musa ‘Truly Tiny’
Compact fruiting banana with deep green foliage. Reaches just 2–3 feet tall, ideal for bright windows, patios, or greenhouse spaces.

💚 Musa ‘Dwarf Green’
Broad, vibrant green leaves with classic tropical appeal in a manageable size. Strong grower and excellent container specime, produces bananas that are green when ripe!.

🍌 Musa ‘Lakatan’
Prized dessert banana known for sweet flavor and vigorous growth. Excellent choice for edible plant collectors. Popular in the Phillipines.

🌿 Musa ‘Popo‘ulu’
Distinctive Hawaiian-type cooking banana with bold structure and beautiful foliage — standout addition to any collection.

Substitutions permitted — please message prior to purchase if you would like a specific substitute.

Healthy, rooted, and ready for the next stage of growth.

item.title: Musa Florida, Variegated Banana - US Seller - WELL ROOTED by PetioleAndBloomLLC
item.description:
99.00 USD

🍌🤍 Variegated Banana Plant
Musa sp. (Variegated)

A rare, collector-grade variegated Musa featuring bold white striping and marbled sectoring across lush green, paddle-shaped leaves. The photos show classic banana growth traits — a developing pseudostem with tightly rolled “cigar” leaves that unfurl into broad tropical blades.

Fast-growing in warmth and bright light, this plant brings instant tropical architecture to patios, greenhouses, and bright indoor spaces — with the added wow factor of unpredictable, one-of-a-kind variegation.

🎁 Petiole & Bloom Welcome Kit
Every plant includes a small starter supplement selected to support healthy establishment and early growth.

✨ What Makes It Exceptional
🤍 True Variegated Musa Foliage
Clean white bands, brushstrokes, and sectoral blocks that vary leaf to leaf and plant to plant.
🌿 Classic Banana Structure
Upright juvenile form with emerging spear leaves and expanding blades as it matures.
🏝 Tropical Statement Plant
Even at starter size, the foliage reads bold and architectural.
🪴 Container-Ready
Ideal for stepping up into larger pots as it grows—perfect for patios and protected outdoor areas.\
📦 Plant Details
• One live, well-rooted Variegated Musa (banana plant)
• Most plants established in 4” nursery pots
• Purple pots are 3” nursery size
• Healthy, actively growing plants comparable to photos
• Variegation is naturally variable; each plant is unique

Photos reflect the actual plants available.

🌱 Care Overview
☀️ Light: Bright light to several hours of direct sun (more light = sturdier growth and better color). Adjust sun exposure to ensure maximum amount of sun without scorching--morning sun is usually best.
💧 Water: Keep evenly moist; do not allow to fully dry out
🌿 Soil: Rich, airy, well-draining mix (tropical blend + perlite/bark recommended)
🌡️ Warmth: Loves heat; protect from cold drafts and temperatures below ~55°F
❄️ Hardiness: Best outdoors in USDA Zones 9–11 (or overwinter indoors elsewhere)

📦 Arrival Tip: Water upon arrival, then keep in bright shade/indirect light for a few days before gradually increasing sun exposure.

item.title: Popoulu Banana Plant (Musa ‘Popoulu’) — Red Trunk Polynesian Tropical Fruit & Foliage by PetioleAndBloomLLC
item.description:
21.99 USD

🍌 Popoulu Banana Plant (Musa Popoulu) — Red Trunk, Tropical Fruit & Foliage

A stunning Pacific Island banana with a vivid red pseudostem, lush tropical leaves, and the potential to produce short, plump, richly flavored bananas with salmon-pink or pinkish-blush pulp. Musa ‘Popoulu’ blends dramatic ornamental appeal, edible fruit potential, and cultural rarity — perfect for patios, greenhouses, edible landscapes, or container gardens.

🎁 Welcome Kit with Every Plant!
We want your Popoulu to thrive! Every plant ships with a caring guidance packet and ongoing support — we’re here if you need help, even after your purchase.

💪 How Our Plants Are Different
Many sellers ship fragile plugs straight from tissue culture. We take a ready-to-grow approach, shipping well-rooted banana plants in quality, enriched soil that are primed for a strong start.
We:
🌱 Dust soil with DYNOMYCO at transplant for healthy root growth
💧 Apply a balanced kelp + nutrient treatment
🦀 Blend in natural, slow-release nutrition to support soil biology
🌿 Condition each plant so it transitions easily into your home or garden
Result: stronger roots, richer foliage color, and easier adaptation than typical freshly acclimated plugs.

🍌 Quick Facts
Botanical Name: Musa Popoulu (Red Trunk Popo’ulu)
Trunk: Bright reddish-maroon pseudostem that stands out in any space
Height: ~8–15+ ft outdoors; smaller in containers
Light: Full sun to bright light
Water: Keep soil moist, well-drained
Temperature: Warm, tropical conditions preferred
Fruit: Short, plump bananas with sweet, apple-like flavor and salmon-pink/pinkish pulp under warm, sunny conditions
Best For: Tropical collectors, edible landscapes, patios, containers

🌴 What Makes Popoulu Special
Bold Red Trunk & Tropical Presence
The most striking feature of this banana is its deep red/maroon pseudostem, providing year-round visual impact in landscapes, patios, or greenhouse collections.
Dual Ornamental + Edible Value
Popoulu is both ornamental and productive. Its lush foliage adds tropical flair, while its short, plump fruit with pink-tinted pulp provides a delicious homegrown treat that’s revered in Polynesian and Pacific Island cultures for flavor.
Pacific Island Heritage & Rarity
This variety is a rare Polynesian heirloom banana with traditional Pacific Island roots, making it a standout choice for collectors and tropical fruit enthusiasts alike.

🌞 Growing Tips
Water immediately upon receipt and keep soil evenly moist.
Acclimate to bright light gradually, then move into full sun if outdoors.
Use a nutrient-rich, well-draining mix and feed during active growth.
Warm, humid conditions help bring out both bold foliage color and fruiting potential.
With the right care — abundant light, warmth, and moisture — Popoulu rewards growers with exotic tropical foliage and delicious, unique bananas that are both beautiful and flavorful.
